name: Text Summarizer
description: Extractive text summarization using TF-based sentence scoring. Selects the most informative sentences and extracts key phrases without requiring any LLM or external dependencies.

maxSentences.description: Maximum number of sentences in the summary (default: 5)

keyPhrases.description: Top key phrases from the text

implementation.code:
import sys
import os
import json
import re
import math
from collections import Counter

STOP_WORDS = frozenset([
    'a', 'an', 'the', 'and', 'or', 'but', 'in', 'on', 'at', 'to', 'for', 'of', 'with',
    'by', 'from', 'is', 'was', 'are', 'were', 'be', 'been', 'being', 'have', 'has', 'had',
    'do', 'does', 'did', 'will', 'would', 'could', 'should', 'may', 'might', 'shall',
    'can', 'need', 'dare', 'ought', 'used', 'it', 'its', 'this', 'that', 'these', 'those',
    'i', 'me', 'my', 'mine', 'we', 'us', 'our', 'ours', 'you', 'your', 'yours',
    'he', 'him', 'his', 'she', 'her', 'hers', 'they', 'them', 'their', 'theirs',
    'what', 'which', 'who', 'whom', 'whose', 'where', 'when', 'how', 'why',
    'not', 'no', 'nor', 'as', 'if', 'then', 'than', 'too', 'very', 'so',
    'just', 'about', 'above', 'after', 'again', 'all', 'also', 'am', 'an', 'any',
    'because', 'before', 'between', 'both', 'each', 'few', 'get', 'got',
    'here', 'into', 'more', 'most', 'much', 'must', 'new', 'now', 'only',
    'other', 'out', 'over', 'own', 'same', 'some', 'still', 'such', 'there',
    'through', 'under', 'up', 'while', 'well', 'also', 'back', 'even', 'however'
])

def tokenize(text):
    """Simple word tokenization."""
    return re.findall(r'[a-zA-Z][\'a-zA-Z]*', text.lower())

def split_sentences(text):
    """Split text into sentences using regex."""
    # Handle common abbreviations to avoid false splits
    text = re.sub(r'\b(Mr|Mrs|Ms|Dr|Prof|Sr|Jr|vs|etc|inc|ltd|co)\.',
                  lambda m: m.group().replace('.', '<DOT>'), text, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    # Split on sentence-ending punctuation followed by space + uppercase or end
    parts = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z"])', text)
    # Restore abbreviation dots
    parts = [p.replace('<DOT>', '.').strip() for p in parts]
    return [s for s in parts if len(s) > 10]

def extract_key_phrases(words, top_n=10):
    """Extract top key phrases by frequency, filtering stop words."""
    content_words = [w for w in words if w not in STOP_WORDS and len(w) > 2]
    freq = Counter(content_words)
    return [word for word, count in freq.most_common(top_n)]

def main():
    inputs = json.loads(os.environ.get('SKILL_INPUTS', '{}'))
    text = inputs.get('text', '')
    max_sentences = inputs.get('maxSentences', 5)

    if not text.strip():
        print(json.dumps({'summary': '', 'keyPhrases': []}))
        return

    sentences = split_sentences(text)
    if not sentences:
        print(json.dumps({'summary': text[:500], 'keyPhrases': []}))
        return

    # Tokenize full text for word frequencies
    all_words = tokenize(text)
    if not all_words:
        print(json.dumps({'summary': sentences[0] if sentences else '', 'keyPhrases': []}))
        return

    # Compute word frequencies (TF), excluding stop words
    word_freq = Counter(w for w in all_words if w not in STOP_WORDS and len(w) > 2)
    if not word_freq:
        # All stop words - just return first sentences
        selected = sentences[:max_sentences]
        print(json.dumps({'summary': ' '.join(selected), 'keyPhrases': []}))
        return

    max_freq = max(word_freq.values())
    # Normalize frequencies
    for word in word_freq:
        word_freq[word] /= max_freq

    # Score each sentence
    sentence_scores = []
    for i, sentence in enumerate(sentences):
        words = tokenize(sentence)
        content_words = [w for w in words if w in word_freq]
        if not content_words:
            score = 0.0
        else:
            score = sum(word_freq[w] for w in content_words) / len(content_words)

        # Boost for position: first and last sentences get a bonus
        if i == 0:
            score *= 1.3
        elif i == len(sentences) - 1:
            score *= 1.1
        elif i < 3:
            score *= 1.15

        # Slight penalty for very long sentences
        if len(words) > 40:
            score *= 0.9

        sentence_scores.append((i, score, sentence))

    # Select top sentences by score
    n = min(max_sentences, len(sentence_scores))
    top = sorted(sentence_scores, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:n]
    # Re-order by original position for coherent reading
    top.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])

    summary = ' '.join(item[2] for item in top)
    key_phrases = extract_key_phrases(all_words, top_n=10)

    print(json.dumps({'summary': summary, 'keyPhrases': key_phrases}))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
